blockingIssues.description: Duplicate Claude source rows still share AI explanation state and cache by logical `skillId` only. `SkillDetailView` reloads and refreshes explanations with `detailRequest.skillId`, while the store/backend explanation APIs and `skill_explanations` table also key by `skill_id` + `lang`, so opening the user copy after generating or caching an explanation for the marketplace copy can surface the wrong explanation instead of preserving source-specific detail state.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated `.factory/library/claude-source-aware-detail-api.md` to record that AI explanation caching remains keyed only by logical `skill_id` + `lang`, so duplicate Claude user/marketplace rows can leak explanation text across sources until the explanation surface becomes row-aware.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: When a feature claims that duplicate Claude row switching updates the full detail surface, explicitly require verification of the AI Explanation tab and any cached row-derived detail state in addition to metadata, markdown content, and management affordances.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The blocking scrutiny failure for `claude-detail-read-only-source-ui` came from explanation state staying keyed only by logical `skillId`, while the current skill guidance and example handoff focus on metadata/content/action switching and never call out the explanation surface.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a reusable macOS native Tauri validation recipe (isolated HOME launch, System Events/window inspection, screenshot capture, and current limitations) or a deterministic automation hook for duplicate-row flows so workers do not rediscover ad hoc native-validation steps each time.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Three reviews in this scrutiny run (`claude-row-id-detail-compatibility-fix`, `claude-platform-list-source-aware-ui`, and `claude-platform-continuity-and-rescan`) independently reported that native Tauri proof still depends on ad hoc System Events / screenshot probing because the shared skill and mission library do not capture a stable recipe.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the clean-tree success checklist for mission-scoped work in already-dirty repositories: workers should leave unrelated changes untouched, verify scoped staged/committed files, and report the remaining pre-existing dirt explicitly instead of implying a globally clean repo is always achievable.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `claude-detail-read-only-source-ui` review noted that the worker had to explain the mismatch manually because mission guidance forbids cleaning unrelated dirty files, while shared worker-base expectations still read like a globally clean tree is required for success.

rejectedObservations.observation: The list UI added `t(..., { defaultValue })` source/read-only labels without new locale JSON keys.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous: project guidance requires user-visible text to go through i18n, but it does not clearly state whether `t()` with `defaultValue` is acceptable without dedicated `en.json` / `zh.json` entries.

rejectedObservations.observation: `.factory/services.yaml`'s shared `test-frontend-targeted` command no longer covers the full Claude platform UX frontend regression surface after `SkillDetailDrawer.test.tsx` and `AppShell.test.tsx` were added.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous: the command name is already referenced by worker skills, and validator-side additive changes cannot safely decide whether the shared default should be replaced or supplemented without orchestrator direction.
